# KVTX Script Vectors

These vectors characterize KVTXScript framing through the shared production codec. They use raw script bytes and expected extracted values so `PolarKvtxScriptCodec` preserves write, append, remove, missing-key, unknown-command, malformed-script, and little-endian integer behavior without relying on platform-specific test construction. `KvtxCommonPolicyTest.kt` now executes the shared production codec for write-and-commit framing, append/remove extraction, EX zero-index and non-empty-index behavior, unsigned key handling, unknown-command stop behavior, typed malformed-script rejection, and `kvtx-readiness.json` behavior-family coverage, while Android and iOS utilities remain compatibility adapters.

`kvtx-readiness.json` names the behavior-family gate that must stay covered after KVTX script code moves into shared: write-and-commit framing, empty-data write framing, unsigned 32-bit key preservation, multiple-key selection, append concatenation, remove clearing, extended write/append/remove zero-index behavior, non-empty extended-index ignore policy, unknown-command stop policy, malformed-script typed error policy, truncated-payload platform vector references, platform KVTX vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate`.
